# <Product / Feature Name> — Product Requirements Document (PRD)

## Document control
| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Version | <e.g. 1.0> |
| Status | <Draft — not approved / Ready for sign-off / Approved> |
| Owner (PO) | <name> |
| Author | <BA / PO> |
| Approvers | <names / roles> |
| Last updated | <date> |
| Quality stamp | score <NN>/100 — band: <band> OR not run |

### Change history
| Version | Date | Summary of changes | Driver |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1.0 | <date> | Initial sign-off document | <answer sheet v1 / —> |
<!-- finalize never overwrites silently: the previous file is archived as
     prd.v<N>.md, Version bumps, and a row is added here. The canonical prd.md
     always holds the latest version. -->

> Assembled from confirmed outputs. Items marked `ASSUMPTION` / `OPEN QUESTION`
> are unresolved. Status is never `Approved` while a blocker-level finding exists.

## Executive summary
<Problem, target user, intended outcome, and the value in 1–2 paragraphs.>

## 0. How to read this document
### 0.1 What this is
<One or two sentences: what this document covers and who it is for.> **Quick read:**
skim the Executive summary, then §3 "How the system works", §5 "Requirements", and
§13 "Sign-off blockers".

### 0.2 Symbol conventions
Codes are **stable across versions**; the names beside them are only to make the
document readable.
| Symbol | Means | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `F0n-Name` | A functional flow (number is the stable anchor; name is for reading) | `F02-Login` |
| `R#` | A product requirement | `R01` |
| `BR#` | A business rule | `BR3` |
| `T-#` | A test scenario | `T-14` |
| `A#` / `Q#` / `S#` | Assumption / Open Question / Suggestion | `A1` / `Q2` / `S1` |

### 0.3 Glossary
Each core term defined once (only terms the requirements actually use).
| Term | Plain meaning |
| --- | --- |
| <core term> | <one-line plain meaning> |

## 1. Goals & success metrics
- Goal: <…>
- Success metric: <measurable target, e.g. +10% activation in 14 days>
- Non-goals: <…>

## 2. Scope
**In scope**
- <…>

**Out of scope**
- <…>

## 3. How the system works (overview)
A plain end-to-end narrative of the journey so a first-time reader understands the
whole before the parts. Detail for each flow lives in §7.
<entry → identify/authenticate → core action → processing → result → follow-up,
told as prose. Compose from the journey; do not invent steps.>

**Main flow diagram** — one representative diagram for the end-to-end journey.
```mermaid
<the single overview diagram from model-suggestions.md>
```
**View / edit:** https://mermaid.live/

## 4. Personas / actors
| Actor | Description | Key permissions |
| --- | --- | --- |

### 4.1 Stakeholder perspectives
Beyond user ↔ system. One row per relevant stakeholder.
| Stakeholder | Need / concern | Consumes / produces |
| --- | --- | --- |
| operations / accounting / reconciliation / partners / risk / maintenance / data / security | <…> | <…> |

## 5. Requirements
**One grouped table (Principle 13.7).** Requirements grouped by capability in journey
order; within a group **Must → Should → Could**. Each is a self-standing sentence
(actor + trigger + observable outcome); **Why** is the business reason, not a
cross-reference. Flow / rule / test / source links live in §12 Traceability. Every
requirement (including deferred) appears once; IDs are stable (append, never renumber).
| ID | Requirement | Why | Priority |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Group A — <capability name>** | | | |
| R01 | When <trigger>, <actor> can <action>, resulting in <outcome>. | <plain business reason> | Must |
| R02 | <…> | <…> | Should |
| **Group B — <capability name>** | | | |
| R03 | <…> | <…> | Must |

## 6. Business rules
| BR id | Rule (or `OPEN QUESTION`) | Effective from / Version | Applies to (new/existing/both) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BR1 | <explicit rule> | <date / vN / n/a> | <new/existing/both> |

### 6.1 Configuration & settings
| Parameter | Config store | Owner | Validation | Effective-dated? | Propagation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

### 6.2 Scheduled jobs / batch
| Job | Schedule | Idempotency/Resume | Inputs | Outputs | Downstream consumers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

## 7. Functional Flows (process-centric)
Organized **by business process** (use case), not by diagram type. Each flow keeps
step-by-step + activity + sequence together for the SAME process. Composed from
`model-suggestions.md`; do not invent steps/rules/errors. Flow IDs are `F0n-Name`
(number stable, name appended).

### 7.1 Flow Catalog
| Flow ID | Business name | Actor(s) | Goal | Diagrams | Related rules (BR) | Related error codes | Requirement (Rxx) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| F01-<Name> | <…> | <…> | <…> | activity + sequence | <…> | <…> | <R01> |

### 7.2 Flow F01-<Name> — <Business name>
**7.2.1 Step-by-step**
| # | Actor | Action | System response | Rule / validation / error code |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | <…> | <…> | <…> | <…> |

**7.2.2 Activity diagram (PlantUML)**
```plantuml
<from model-suggestions.md — THIS flow>
```
**View / edit:** https://www.plantuml.com/plantuml — ref: https://plantuml.com/

**7.2.3 Sequence diagram (Mermaid)** — same flow
```mermaid
<from model-suggestions.md — THIS flow>
```
**View / edit:** https://mermaid.live/
<!-- Simple/single-system flow: replace with "Sequence diagram not required — <reason>". -->

**7.2.4 Gaps revealed / Open questions**
- <gap or `OPEN QUESTION` from this flow>

### 7.3 Flow F02-<Name> — <Business name>
<repeat; activity + sequence in one block must be the SAME process — never mix>

### 7.4 Screen / Display Matrix
Placed **after** the flows. Reference each screen's flow as `F0n-Name / step`.
| Screen | Flow / Step | Purpose | Display fields | User actions | Validation | Error / empty / loading states | UX / content notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

### 7.5 Screen Wireframes (low-fidelity HTML)
Render an inline HTML visualization widget here when the target Markdown renderer
supports it; otherwise write `clarify-output/wireframes.html` and link it here.
Use `templates/wireframe-template.html`. The widget is grayscale, derive-only, and
maps every screen back to its source flow/step and requirement. Do not use ASCII
wireframes as the primary screen artifact.

## 8. Data & API impact
<From api-data-impact.md when build-ready layer exists; otherwise summarize
business/product-level affected systems from the draft and mark detailed API/data
impact as optional for Dev/QA handoff.>

## 9. Edge cases, exception handling & messages
<Summary from edge-case-matrix.md.>

### 9.1 Error Handling & Message Mapping
Each error maps back to the `F0n-Name / step` it occurs in (from §7).
| Error code | Flow / Step | Scenario | HTTP / API status | Entity state | Transaction / operation state | User-facing message | Retryable? | Required action | Needs Ops/CS? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| Model type | State set | Trigger / event | Owner system | Terminal states | Gaps |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Entity state | <from model-suggestions.md> | <who/what moves it> | <source of truth> | <…> | <…> |
| Transaction / operation state | <from model-suggestions.md> | <…> | <…> | <…> | <…> |

## 12. Traceability summary
<Include when `traceability-matrix.md` exists. If not, state: "Detailed
story/AC/test traceability was not generated; run `/clarify:from-spec` only if
Dev/QA build-ready handoff is needed.">
Every requirement (including deferred) appears once and links to its flow, rule,
error/state, and **Source** (the `A#/BR#/S#/Q#` it came from). Screen mapping optional.
| R id | Flow (F0n-Name) | Business rule | Error / state | Source (← A#/BR#/S#/Q#) | Story id | AC | Test id | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

### 13.2 Open Questions

### 13.3 Suggested additional capabilities (for confirmation)
- S1 — SUGGESTION: <capability> — rationale: <why>.

## Appendix: Artifact index (source)
Companion files produced in `clarify-output/` (list only those that exist; diagrams
are **source code** rendered via the linked viewers — no image files are produced).
| Artifact | Description | Path |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Draft | Shaped PRD draft | `clarify-output/prd-draft.md` |
| Audit report | Score, band, findings | `clarify-output/audit-report.md` |
| Edge-case matrix | Edge / negative / boundary / temporal / batch | `clarify-output/edge-case-matrix.md` |
| Error handling | Error → state → user-message → action map | `clarify-output/error-handling.md` |
| Model suggestions | Per-flow activity (PlantUML) + sequence (Mermaid) + state | `clarify-output/model-suggestions.md` |
| Wireframes | Low-fi HTML screen wireframes | `clarify-output/wireframes.html` |
| API/data impact | Entities, source of truth, endpoints | `clarify-output/api-data-impact.md` |
| Stories + AC | User stories and acceptance criteria | `clarify-output/stories.md` |
| Test scenarios | Test cases | `clarify-output/test-scenarios.md` |
| Traceability | Requirement ↔ flow ↔ rule ↔ error ↔ source ↔ story ↔ test | `clarify-output/traceability-matrix.md` |
| HTML PRD | Full PRD rendered from `prd.md` | `clarify-output/prd.html` |
